headline: Expense Submissions Doubled, Employee Workflows Regressing — Investigate WFM 1
summary: Week of Apr 28–May 4: addExpense_upserted more than doubled (+111%, 674 vs 320) and expenseDetail_upserted surged +107% — the strongest expense week in Atlas history. AI extraction adoption is compounding. On the risk side: employee_submitted dropped 31% and employee_created fell 43% with no WFM 1 PR explaining the drop. SSO failures rose 31% alongside MFA's 135% adoption spike — needs investigation before MFA widens. All pods rolled to new cycles this week. Exceptions continued falling to 2,271 (down another 42%).
weekSignalReason: Record expense metrics and continued exception improvement, but two unexplained regressions (WFM 1 employee workflows, SSO failures) need investigation this week.
execSignal: AMBER — expense product metrics at all-time high, but employee submission and creation drops require root cause analysis before they compound.
p1Actions: Investigate employee_submitted (−31%) and employee_created (−43%) in WFM 1 — no shipped PR explains the drop. Check census verification flow (WFM1-1112) for broken save paths.
Cross-reference sso_login_failed spike (+31% to 174) against MFA rollout — confirm PAY-1093 refactor is not causing auth failures for specific country codes or device types.
Confirm FNM2-983 (GTN Acceleration to PROD) shipped with PostHog observability — mfa_sms_setup_phone surge suggests real production traffic but GTN still has zero telemetry.

regressions.hypothesis: WFM1-1112 re-architected the assignee/flagged-field flow last week. Possible the save trigger path changed or a form step was broken in the process. No new WFM 1 PR this week to explain it.
Correlated with employee_submitted drop — both WFM 1 lifecycle events declining together suggests a flow disruption upstream (e.g. broken onboarding step or form validation regression).
Feedback submission dropped 80% while banner impressions rose 37%. Users are seeing the AI extraction prompt but not engaging with the feedback form. Possible UX change hid the submission step.

topRisks.title: SSO failures up 31% alongside MFA rollout
topRisks.reason: sso_login_failed rose from 133 to 174 while mfa_sms_setup_phone surged from 17 to 40. Correlation is high. MFA auth surface changes can create edge-case failures that don't show in overall success rate (still 93%) but affect specific user segments.
topRisks.recommendedAction: Pull sso_login_failed breakdown by error code and country in PostHog. Compare against pre-MFA baseline.

topRisks.title: WFM 1 employee lifecycle regression — second week risk
topRisks.reason: If employee_submitted and employee_created stay down in week 2 of cycle 27, this becomes a confirmed regression affecting WSE onboarding and task completion. Window to catch it is now.
topRisks.recommendedAction: QA the employee creation and submission flow in staging this week. Check for any silent JS errors introduced by WFM1-1112.

topRisks.title: AI expense feedback loop breaking
topRisks.reason: addExpense_aiExpenseExtractionFeedback dropped 80% while banner impressions are up 37%. The feedback mechanism is either hidden or broken — losing the signal that trains and validates the AI extraction model.
topRisks.recommendedAction: Check whether the feedback button/form is still visible in the current expense extraction UI. This is WFM 2's quality signal.

instrumentationGaps: FNM2-983 (GTN Acceleration to PROD) — confirm PostHog events were added before last week's rollout.
MFA flow — mfa_sms_setup_phone tracks setup initiation but there is no event for MFA completion or failure reason. Add mfa_completed and mfa_failed_reason to understand the 31% SSO failure increase.

WFM1 employee lifecycle — employee_submitted and employee_created are the only signals. No step-level funnel (employee_form_started, employee_form_step_completed) to diagnose where the 31-43% drop is occurring.

opportunities.idea: Expense AI extraction is having a breakout week — addExpense_upserted at 674 is 2x the previous record. Now is the time to build the feedback loop dashboard and measure AI extraction accuracy vs manual corrections.
MFA setup surged 135% (40 vs 17). Capture a completion funnel: setup_started, otp_sent, otp_verified, mfa_enabled. This data will be essential for the PAY cycle 27 MFA review.
Pageviews up 15% and first_click up 24% — platform engagement is growing. Identify the top entry pages driving the first_click surge before attributing it to a specific feature.
